messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**
Tarsus what is the incorruptible and why and how is incurruptible?

> **Thinking Process:**
> 
> The user asks about "the incorruptible" and its nature. This is a theological question about a key NT concept. I need to ground this in Daniel Miles' teachings and the AKJV text. The concept of "incorruptible" (aphthartos) is central to the resurrection (1 Cor 15:52-54), the inheritance of the saints (1 Pet 1:4), and the seed of the Word (1 Pet 1:23). I must contrast this with the "corruptible" (phthartos) nature of the old creation, the law, and the flesh. The user's profile shows interest in Christology and progression from law to grace, so I will connect incorruptibility to the grace-life in Christ, not to any human effort or law-keeping. I will use the provided documents where relevant, but the core answer must come from the scripture and the theological framework of the corpus. The documents provided are mostly about social media, branding, and conspiracy theories, which are not directly relevant to this question. However, I can use the general principle from the corpus that the "incorruptible" is the nature of the new creation in Christ, received by grace through faith, not by works. I will cite the relevant passages from the AKJV and the theological framework of the GodShew corpus. I will avoid placing the believer "under" anything, even "under incorruptibility." Instead, the believer is "in" Christ, who is the incorruptible one, and partakes of His nature. The "how" is by the seed of the Word (1 Pet 1:23) and the resurrection power of Christ (1 Cor 15). The "why" is because the old creation is passing away, and God's purpose is to bring many sons to glory (Heb 2:10) in an eternal, incorruptible state. I will use footnotes to reference the key scriptural passages and the theological principles from the corpus.

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

The term "incorruptible" (Greek: *aphthartos*) in the NT scripture points to the very nature of the new creation in Christ Jesus, a quality that is not merely the absence of decay but the positive, eternal, and divine life that is the inheritance of every believer who is in Him [^1]. The "why" of incorruptibility is rooted in the character of God Himself, who is the "incorruptible God" (Rom 1:23), and in the finished work of His Son. The old creation, the cOLD covenant of law, and the flesh are all "corruptible" (*phthartos*), subject to decay, death, and the bondage of sin (Rom 8:21). Why? Because the law could only produce a righteousness of works, which is inherently corruptible, being of the dust. But the grace of God in JC produces a righteousness that is eternal, a life that is "incorruptible" because it is the very life of the risen Christ imparted to the believer [^2]. The inheritance reserved in heaven for us is described as "incorruptible, and undefiled, and that fadeth not away" (1 Pet 1:4), directly contrasting with the earthly, temporal, and fading glory of the law-system.

The "how" of incorruptibility is a matter of divine seed and resurrection power, not human striving. The Apostle Peter declares that we are "born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and abideth for ever" (1 Pet 1:23). This is the mechanism: the Word of truth, the gospel of grace, is the incorruptible seed that, when received by faith, imparts the very nature of God into the human spirit. This is not a process of self-improvement or law-keeping; it is a divine genesis. Furthermore, the resurrection of JC is the guarantee and pattern of this incorruptibility. Paul explains in 1 Corinthians 15:52-54 that "this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality." This is a future event for the body, but the spirit of the believer is already "in Christ," who is the resurrection and the life (Jn 11:25). The "how" is therefore entirely a work of grace: we are "in" the Incorruptible One, and His life becomes our life [^3].

This innerstanding moves the believer from the realm of the "corruptible" — which is the realm of the law, the flesh, and the world's system — into the realm of the "incorruptible," which is the Kingdom of God. The law, being administered through the ministry of condemnation (2 Cor 3:9), could only produce a righteousness that was subject to the corruption of the flesh. But grace, ministered by the Spirit of the living God, produces the fruit of the Spirit, which is eternal and incorruptible. The believer is not "under" incorruptibility as a new law; rather, the believer is "with" Christ, who is the Incorruptible One. This is a union of life, not a system of control. The "why" is that God's purpose from before the foundation of the world was to have a family of sons and daughters partaking of His own divine nature (2 Pet 1:4), and the "how" is solely through the incorruptible seed of His Word and the power of His resurrection, received by grace through faith [^4].

Learn More:
- [God Of All Grace](/pages/godgrace)
- [The Incorruptible Seed](/pages/incorruptible_seed)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

1.title: God Of All Grace
1.snippet: Grace is the divine enablement and the very life of God imparted to the believer. It is not a thing, but a Person—Jesus Christ. This life is eternal, incorruptible, and cannot be touched by sin or death.

2.title: The Incorruptible Seed
2.snippet: The word of God is the incorruptible seed. When it is sown in the heart of a believer, it produces the very nature of God. This is not a human work; it is a divine generation. The law could only produce corruptible fruit, but the gospel produces the incorruptible life of Christ.

3.title: Resurrection Life
3.snippet: The resurrection of Jesus Christ is not merely a historical event; it is the pattern and power of the believer's new life. We are raised with Him to walk in newness of life, a life that is incorruptible because He is incorruptible.

4.title: 2 Peter 1:4 Study
4.snippet: Whereby are given unto us exceeding great and precious promises: that by these ye might be partakers of the divine nature, having escaped the corruption that is in the world through lust. This partaking is not by law, but by the promises of grace.
